skill.name: format-standardize
skill.description: Standardize formatting and apply consistent style to the deliverable. Use after generation to ensure the output matches the user's formatting standards and conventions.
skill.summary: Standardize formatting and apply consistent style to the deliverable. Use after generation to ensure...
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and weak cryptography, but review found these are false positives in a Markdown-only instruction file. The flagged external command locations are fenced JavaScript and output examples, and the weak crypto alert at SKILL.md line 3 has no cryptographic operation. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged locations are Markdown fenced examples that show JavaScript formatting and sample output. They do not instruct Ruby backtick execution or execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is visible in SKILL.md as Markdown fences and illustrative JavaScript snippets. No shell command invocation, Ruby code, or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Description Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 3 is plain frontmatter describing formatting standardization. It contains no hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is descriptive metadata only, and the file contains no cryptographic code. The alert is consistent with a substring match rather than a semantic security issue.

content.value_statement: Teams often receive useful output with uneven formatting, headings, spacing, or code style. This skill applies stated standards and returns a polished deliverable ready for review.
actual_capabilities: Reviews generated deliverables for formatting consistency.
Applies user or project formatting standards when available.
Standardizes code indentation, naming, spacing, imports, and punctuation.
Improves Markdown headers, lists, links, tables, and code block formatting.
Summarizes standards used, changes made, and validation checks.
limitations: Does not define a new style guide when no preference is provided.
Does not guarantee semantic correctness of code or prose.
Depends on available project standards and formatter tools.
May need user review for subjective tone or publication requirements.

use_cases.title: Prepare Code for Review
use_cases.description: Apply consistent indentation, spacing, imports, and semicolon style before a pull request.

use_cases.title: Polish Project Documentation
use_cases.description: Standardize headings, lists, links, tables, and code blocks in Markdown documentation.

use_cases.title: Normalize Generated Content
use_cases.description: Bring AI-generated reports, explanations, or handoffs into a consistent structure and tone.

prompt_templates.title: Format This Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the format-standardize skill on this deliverable. Apply consistent headings, lists, spacing, and punctuation.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Project Standards
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the format-standardize skill and apply this project's saved standards. Report the standards used and the formatting changes made.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the format-standardize skill to prepare this code and documentation for review. Standardize imports, indentation, Markdown, and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Consistency Across Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the format-standardize skill to audit this full deliverable for formatting drift. Normalize section structure, code style, tables, terminology, and validation notes.

output_examples.input: A draft README has uneven heading levels, mixed bullet styles, and inconsistent spacing.
output_examples.output: The README is returned with aligned heading hierarchy, consistent bullets, normalized spacing, and a brief list of formatting changes.

output_examples.input: A React component uses inconsistent parameter spacing, JSX spacing, and semicolon usage.
output_examples.output: The component is returned with consistent React formatting, standardized expression spacing, and matching semicolon style.

output_examples.input: A generated implementation summary mixes prose style, checklist formatting, and validation sections.
output_examples.output: The summary is returned with a consistent section order, parallel list structure, and a concise validation section.

best_practices: Provide the target style guide or formatter preference before using the skill.
Run the skill after content generation, not before the deliverable exists.
Review the final output for meaning, not only formatting.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to change product requirements or technical decisions.
Applying conflicting formatting standards in the same request.
Treating formatting cleanup as a substitute for tests or factual review.

faq.question: Does this skill execute formatters automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes formatting checks and may use available tools only when the environment and user request allow it.

faq.question: Can it format both code and prose?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers code, Markdown documentation, refactoring explanations, test suites, and general content.

faq.question: Does it create a new style guide?
faq.answer: No. It applies existing user or project standards when available and otherwise uses consistent conventional formatting.

faq.question: Is it useful with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for post-generation cleanup.

faq.question: Will it verify program behavior?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on formatting consistency and presentation, not functional correctness.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after drafting code, documentation, reports, or summaries that need consistent style before review or delivery.
